commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `optimizeImageToJpeg` to return the original source buffer with zero resize/quality metadata after every JPEG optimization attempt fails.

Reproducibility: yes. A high-confidence source-path reproduction exists: force `resizeToJpeg` to throw a guard or non-JPEG optimizer error, and this PR returns the original bytes from `optimizeImageToJpeg` before the caller applies only byte-cap and JPEG content-type mapping.
commands.review_followup: Contributor rework and real behavior proof are required, and automation cannot supply proof from the contributor's runtime while the paired issue remains open.

target.title: fix(media): fallback to original buffer when image optimization fails
target.body:
## Problem
`optimizeImageToJpeg()` throws "Failed to optimize image" when all resize/quality combinations fail, blocking image analysis entirely.

## Solution
Return the original buffer as fallback instead of throwing. This allows images to reach VLM models even when optimization fails.

## Changes
- Replace `throw new Error("Failed to optimize image")` with return of original buffer
- Set `resizeSide: 0` and `quality: 0` to indicate no optimization was applied

## Testing
- Images that fail optimization now pass through to VLM models
- Backward compatible: successful optimizations still return optimized data

Fixes #73424
target.author: hijirii
